questions.question: Which Zoom feature allows a host to save the meeting files directly onto their own computer hardware?

answerOptions.text: Local Recording
answerOptions.rationale: This feature processes the video data and saves the resulting file to the user's hard drive or SSD.

answerOptions.text: Cloud Recording
answerOptions.rationale: This option stores the meeting data on the service provider's remote servers rather than the user's physical device.

answerOptions.text: Instant Streaming
answerOptions.rationale: Streaming refers to the live broadcast of data and does not inherently imply the storage of a file on a local disk.

answerOptions.text: Cache Recording
answerOptions.rationale: Cache is used for temporary data storage to speed up processes, not for the permanent storage of meeting files.

questions.hint: Identify the option that refers to storage on your physical device rather than a remote server.

questions.question: What visual indicator is displayed in the Zoom interface to notify all participants that the session is being recorded?

answerOptions.text: A red circle icon and a status message in the top-left corner
answerOptions.rationale: Zoom provides a clear visual signal to ensure all participants are aware of the recording for privacy and transparency.

answerOptions.text: A yellow banner that flashes every $5$ minutes
answerOptions.rationale: Flashing banners would be distracting to the meeting flow and are not the standard UI indicator used by the platform.

answerOptions.text: A watermark that appears over the host's video feed
answerOptions.rationale: Watermarks are typically used for branding or security and do not serve as the primary active recording status indicator.

answerOptions.text: A persistent popup window that must be clicked to close
answerOptions.rationale: While a disclaimer may appear at the start, a persistent popup would interfere with the visibility of shared content.

questions.hint: Look for a common UI symbol often used in electronics to indicate an active recording.

questions.question: If a participant who is not the host wants to record the meeting locally, what action must the host take?

answerOptions.text: Grant recording permission to that specific participant
answerOptions.rationale: The host maintains control over who can capture the session and must manually enable this capability for others.

answerOptions.text: Provide the participant with the meeting's encryption key
answerOptions.rationale: Encryption keys are handled automatically by the software for security and are not related to user recording permissions.

answerOptions.text: Assign the participant the 'Waiting Room' manager role
answerOptions.rationale: Managing the waiting room is a logistical task that does not grant rights to save video files to a local drive.

answerOptions.text: No action is needed as all participants can record by default
answerOptions.rationale: For privacy reasons, recording rights are restricted to the host unless specifically delegated to someone else.

questions.hint: Consider how a host manages the specific capabilities of individual attendees during a live session.

questions.question: What is the functional difference between clicking 'Pause' and clicking 'Stop' during a recording session?

answerOptions.text: Pausing results in a single continuous file, while stopping creates a new file if recording resumes
answerOptions.rationale: Stopping the recording terminates the current file process, whereas pausing simply suspends the input into the current file.

answerOptions.text: Pausing mutes the audio, while stopping turns off the video feed
answerOptions.rationale: Audio and video controls are separate from the recording functions and are managed via the microphone and camera buttons.

answerOptions.text: Stopping automatically uploads the file to the web, while pausing keeps it on the CPU
answerOptions.rationale: The upload process typically begins only after the meeting has fully ended, regardless of whether stop or pause was used.

answerOptions.text: There is no functional difference; both buttons perform the same action
answerOptions.rationale: These buttons provide different levels of control over how the final output files are organized and segmented.

questions.hint: Think about how these actions affect the number of separate video files generated at the end of the meeting.

questions.question: Which file format is the standard output for a Zoom video recording?

answerOptions.text: .mp4
answerOptions.rationale: This format is widely used because it offers high compatibility across different operating systems and media players.

answerOptions.text: .exe
answerOptions.rationale: This extension is used for executable programs, not for multimedia video or audio content.

answerOptions.text: .pdf
answerOptions.rationale: This format is intended for document exchange and cannot encapsulate the temporal data of a video stream.

answerOptions.text: .zip
answerOptions.rationale: While recordings can be compressed into this format later, it is an archive container rather than a video playback format.

questions.hint: Consider the most common file extension used for digital video playback on the web.

questions.question: What happens immediately after a meeting ends if a 'Local Recording' was in progress?

answerOptions.text: The recording file is converted into a viewable format
answerOptions.rationale: Zoom must process the raw data captured during the meeting to finalize the video file for the user.

answerOptions.text: The file is automatically emailed to all invited participants
answerOptions.rationale: Automated distribution of local files would consume significant bandwidth and may violate the host's privacy preferences.

answerOptions.text: The computer performs a mandatory system reboot to save the data
answerOptions.rationale: Modern software handles file writing through the operating system without requiring a hardware restart.

answerOptions.text: The recording is deleted if it exceeds $1$ gigabyte in size
answerOptions.rationale: Local recordings are limited only by the available space on the host's hard drive, not an arbitrary software cap.

questions.hint: Think about the necessary processing step required to turn raw meeting data into a standard video file.

questions.question: Where can a host manage and share their 'Cloud Recordings' after the meeting has concluded?

answerOptions.text: The Zoom Web Portal
answerOptions.rationale: The portal serves as the centralized management interface for cloud-based storage, settings, and sharing links.

answerOptions.text: The Windows Task Manager
answerOptions.rationale: This system tool is used for monitoring active processes and hardware performance, not for managing cloud files.

answerOptions.text: The computer's BIOS settings
answerOptions.rationale: BIOS manages low-level hardware initialization and is unrelated to application-level cloud storage services.

answerOptions.text: Only through a third-party social media account
answerOptions.rationale: While files can be uploaded elsewhere, the primary management tools are built directly into the Zoom ecosystem.

questions.hint: Identify the online dashboard where users log in to adjust their account settings and view saved data.

questions.question: Is it possible to generate a separate audio-only file during a Zoom recording session?

answerOptions.text: Yes, Zoom automatically creates an M4A audio file for every recording
answerOptions.rationale: Providing a separate audio file is a standard feature that allows for easier transcription or podcast-style distribution.

answerOptions.text: No, the audio is permanently embedded in the video file and cannot be separated
answerOptions.rationale: While audio is part of the video, the software is designed to output multiple file types simultaneously for user convenience.

answerOptions.text: Only if the host uses an external digital recorder
answerOptions.rationale: The Zoom application itself includes the functionality to capture and save the audio stream independently.

answerOptions.text: Only if all cameras in the meeting are turned off
answerOptions.rationale: The generation of an audio file is independent of whether video signals are being received or recorded.

questions.hint: Consider if the software provides flexibility for users who only need the sound from a session.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the 'Automatic Recording' setting?

answerOptions.text: It begins recording as soon as the meeting starts
answerOptions.rationale: This automation ensures that no part of the meeting is missed by removing the need for a manual start by the host.

answerOptions.text: It records only when the software detects human speech
answerOptions.rationale: This would lead to fragmented files and is not a standard behavior of the recording automation feature.

answerOptions.text: It automatically records the host's entire desktop screen
answerOptions.rationale: Recording captures the meeting interface or shared windows specifically, rather than the entire desktop unless shared.

answerOptions.text: It deletes the recording automatically after it has been viewed once
answerOptions.rationale: Storage management and auto-deletion are separate features from the initial capture settings.

questions.hint: Think about a setting that triggers an action without requiring user intervention at the start of a call.

questions.question: Which of the following determines the availability of the 'Cloud Recording' feature for a specific user?

answerOptions.text: The type of Zoom subscription plan the user has
answerOptions.rationale: Cloud storage involves ongoing costs for the provider and is typically reserved for paid or 'Licensed' account tiers.

answerOptions.text: The physical weight of the user's laptop
answerOptions.rationale: Hardware mass has no impact on the software's ability to communicate with remote cloud servers.

answerOptions.text: The number of monitors connected to the computer
answerOptions.rationale: Display configuration affects visibility but does not change the feature set of the user's account.

answerOptions.text: The specific time of day the meeting takes place
answerOptions.rationale: Cloud services are available $24/7$ and are not restricted by time-based quotas for standard accounts.

questions.hint: Recall which account status usually distinguishes basic free users from professional or business users.
